A **fiscal year** (also known as a **financial year**, or sometimes **budget year**) is a one-[year](/wiki/Year "Year") time interval whose beginning and end may be shifted with respect to the [calendar year](/wiki/Calendar_year "Calendar year") (1 January to 31 December).
In the [Northern Hemisphere](/wiki/Northern_Hemisphere "Northern Hemisphere"), the most common shifted fiscal year is July to the next June, but other practices exist, such as April to the next March. For countries in the [Southern Hemisphere](/wiki/Southern_Hemisphere "Southern Hemisphere"), the fiscal year normally coincides with the calendar year, January to December.
The series of consecutive fiscal years forms a [calendar system](/wiki/Calendar_system "Calendar system") known as **fiscal calendar**.
The names of fiscal years are often shortened based on the calendar year in which they *end*; for example, "FY24" is an abbreviation for "fiscal year 2023-2024".

It is used in [governmental accounting](/wiki/Governmental_accounting "Governmental accounting"), which varies between countries, and for budget purposes. It is also used for [financial reporting](/wiki/Financial_report "Financial report") by businesses and other organizations. Laws in many [jurisdictions](/wiki/Jurisdictions "Jurisdictions") require company financial reports to be prepared and published on an annual basis. Taxation laws generally require accounting records to be maintained and taxes calculated on an annual basis, which usually corresponds to the fiscal year used for government purposes. The calculation of tax on an annual basis is especially relevant for [direct taxes](/wiki/Direct_taxes "Direct taxes"), such as income tax. Many annual government fees—such as council tax and license fees—are also levied on a fiscal year basis, but others are charged on an [anniversary](/wiki/Anniversary "Anniversary") basis.

Some companies, such as [Cisco Systems](/wiki/Cisco_Systems "Cisco Systems"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) end their fiscal year on the same day of the week each year: the day that is closest to a particular date (for example, the Friday closest to 31 December). Under such a system, some fiscal years have 52 weeks and others 53 weeks.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The calendar year is used as the fiscal year by about 65% of publicly traded companies in the United States and for most large corporations in the United Kingdom.[[3]](#cite_note-3) That is the case in many countries around the world with a few exceptions such as Australia, New Zealand, and Japan.[[4]](#cite_note-Fiscal_Year-4)

Many universities have a fiscal year which ends during the summer to align the fiscal year with the [academic year](/wiki/Academic_year "Academic year"), and universities are typically quieter during the summer months. In a similar fashion, many nonprofit performing arts organizations will have a fiscal year which ends during the summer, so that their performance season that begins in the fall and ends in the spring will be within one fiscal year.

Some media/communication-based organizations use a [broadcast calendar](/wiki/Broadcast_calendar "Broadcast calendar") as the basis for their fiscal year.

## Tax year

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Tax year")]

The fiscal year for individuals and entities to report and pay income taxes is often known as the taxpayer's tax year or taxable year. Taxpayers in many jurisdictions may choose their tax year.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Some federal countries, such as Canada and Switzerland, require the provincial or cantonal tax year to align with the federal year. In the United States, most states retained a 30 June fiscal year-end date when the federal government switched to 30 September in 1976. Nearly all jurisdictions require that the tax year be 12 months or 52/53 weeks.[[7]](#cite_note-USC441-7) However, short years are permitted as the first year or when changing tax years.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Most countries require all individuals to pay income tax based on the calendar year. Significant exceptions include:

* Australia: individuals pay income tax based on the financial year of 1 July until 30 June.[[9]](#cite_note-ASIC-9)
* United Kingdom: the tax year for individuals begins on 6 April. This is due to Britain historically having a calendar year starting on [Lady Day](/wiki/Quarter_days#In_England "Quarter days") (25 March) in the [Julian calendar](/wiki/Julian_calendar "Julian calendar") but a fiscal year ending on that day. When the UK [adopted the Gregorian calendar](/wiki/Calendar_(New_Style)_Act_1750 "Calendar (New Style) Act 1750") in 1752, 25 March translated to 5 April and 26 March to 6 April. (See [History of taxation in the United Kingdom#Start of tax year](/wiki/History_of_taxation_in_the_United_Kingdom#Start_of_tax_year "History of taxation in the United Kingdom") for more detailed explanation.)
* United States: individuals may (but rarely do) elect any tax year, subject to IRS approval.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Many jurisdictions require that the tax year conform to the taxpayer's fiscal year for financial reporting. The United States is a notable exception: taxpayers may choose any tax year, but must keep books and records for such year.[[7]](#cite_note-USC441-7)

## Operation by jurisdiction

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Operation by jurisdiction")]

In some jurisdictions, particularly those that permit [tax consolidation](/wiki/Tax_consolidation "Tax consolidation"), companies that are part of a [group](/wiki/Business_group "Business group") of businesses must use nearly the same fiscal year (differences of up to three months are permitted in some jurisdictions, such as the US and Japan), with consolidating entries to adjust for transactions between units with different fiscal years, so the same resources will not be counted more than once or not at all.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Afghanistan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Afghanistan")]

In [Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan "Afghanistan"), from 2011 to 2021, the fiscal year began on 1 [Hamal](/wiki/Farvardin "Farvardin") (20th or 21 March).[[11]](#cite_note-11) The fiscal year aligned with the Persian or [Solar Hijri calendar](/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar "Solar Hijri calendar") used in Afghanistan at the time.

### Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Australia")]

In [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), a fiscal year is commonly called a "financial year" (FY), and starts on 1 July, ending on 30 June the next year. Financial years are designated by the calendar year of the second half of the period. For example, financial year 2026 is the 12-month period ending on 30 June 2026 and can be referred to as FY2025/26. It is used for official purposes, by individual taxpayers and by the overwhelming majority of business enterprises.[[9]](#cite_note-ASIC-9) Business enterprises may opt to use a financial year that ends at the end of a week (e.g., 52 or 53 weeks in length, and therefore is not exactly one calendar year in length), or opt for its financial year to end on a date that matches the reporting cycle of its foreign parent. All entities within the one group must use the same financial year.

For government accounting and budget purposes, pre-[Federation](/wiki/Federation_of_Australia "Federation of Australia") colonies changed the financial year from the calendar year to a year ending 30 June on the following dates: Victoria changed in 1870, South Australia in 1874, Queensland in 1875, Western Australia in 1892, New South Wales in 1895 and Tasmania in 1904. The Commonwealth adopted the near-ubiquitous financial year standard since its inception in 1901.[[12]](#cite_note-Arndt_1990-12) The reason given for the change was for convenience, as Parliament typically sits during May and June, while it was difficult for it to meet in November and December to pass a budget.[[12]](#cite_note-Arndt_1990-12)

The financial year is split into four quarters which cover the following periods:[[13]](#cite_note-13)

| Quarter | Period covered |
| --- | --- |
| Quarter 1 | 1 Jul – 30 Sep |
| Quarter 2 | 1 Oct – 31 Dec |
| Quarter 3 | 1 Jan – 31 Mar |
| Quarter 4 | 1 Apr – 30 Jun |

### Austria

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Austria")]

In [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

### Bangladesh

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Bangladesh")]

In [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh "Bangladesh"), the fiscal year is 1 July to the next 30 June.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

### Belarus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Belarus")]

In [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Brazil

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Brazil")]

In [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

### Bulgaria

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Bulgaria")]

In [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December, both for personal income tax[[16]](#cite_note-16) and for corporate taxes.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Canada")]

In [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), the government's financial year is 1 April to 31 March.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
  
(Q1 1 April – 30 June, Q2 1 July – 30 Sept, Q3 1 Oct – 31 Dec and Q4 1 Jan – 31 Mar)

For individual taxpayers, the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

### China

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: China")]

In [China](/wiki/China "China"), the fiscal year for all entities is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December, and applies to the tax year, statutory year, and planning year.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

### Colombia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Colombia")]

In [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

### Costa Rica

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Costa Rica")]

In [Costa Rica](/wiki/Costa_Rica "Costa Rica"), the fiscal year is the calendar year. January to December. As of 2019 when the tax laws changed.
[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Egypt

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Egypt")]

In [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt"), the fiscal year is 1 July to 30 June.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

### France

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: France")]

In [France](/wiki/France "France"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December, and has been since at least 1911.[[22]](#cite_note-McKenna-22)

### Germany

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Germany")]

In Germany, the fiscal year runs from 1 January until 31 December.

### Greece

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Greece")]

In [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

### Hong Kong

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Hong Kong")]

In [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong"), the government's financial year runs from 1 April to 31 March.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

However, a company incorporated in Hong Kong can determine its own financial year-end, which may be different from the government fiscal year.

### India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: India")]

In [India](/wiki/India "India"), the government's financial year runs from 1 April to 31 March the following year.[[24]](#cite_note-24) The financial year from 1 April 2025 to 31 March 2026 would generally be abbreviated as FY 2025–26 or( FY25-26) ( FY2025/26),(FY2025/2026),(FY25/26), but it may also be called FY 2026 or FY26 on the basis of the ending year.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Companies following the Indian Depositary Receipt (IDR) are given freedom to choose their financial year. For example, Standard Chartered's IDR follows the UK calendar despite being listed in India. Companies following Indian fiscal year get to know their economic health on 31 March of every Indian financial or fiscal year.

The current fiscal year was adopted by the colonial British government in 1867 to align India's financial year with that of the British Empire.[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27) Prior to 1867, India followed a fiscal year that ran from 1 May to 30 April.[[28]](#cite_note-FT-28)

On 4 May 2017, [Madhya Pradesh](/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh") announced that it would move to a January–December financial year, becoming the first Indian state to do so. But later it dropped the idea due to many financial and accounting errors.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

### Indonesia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Indonesia")]

In [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia"), since 2001, the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December. Until 2000, the fiscal year ran from 1 April to 31 March; fiscal year 2000 ran from 1 April to 31 December.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

### Iran

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Iran")]

In [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran"), the fiscal year usually starts on 21st or 22 March (1st of [Farvardin](/wiki/Farvardin "Farvardin") in the [Solar Hejri calendar](/wiki/Solar_Hejri_calendar "Solar Hejri calendar")) and concludes on next year's 20th or 21 March (29th or 30th of [Esfand](/wiki/Esfand "Esfand") in the [Solar Hijri calendar](/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar "Solar Hijri calendar")).[[31]](#cite_note-31)

### Ireland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Ireland")]

In [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December. Until 2001, it was the year ending 5 April, as in the United Kingdom, but was changed with the introduction of the [euro](/wiki/Euro "Euro"). The 2001 tax year was nine months, from April to December.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

### Israel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Israel")]

In [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[33]](#cite_note-33)

### Italy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Italy")]

In [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December. It was changed in 1965, before which it was 1 July to 30 June.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

### Japan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Japan")]

In [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), the government's financial year is from 1 April to 31 March.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

Japan's [income tax](/wiki/Income_tax "Income tax") year is 1 January to 31 December,[[36]](#cite_note-36) but [corporate tax](/wiki/Corporate_tax "Corporate tax") is charged according to the corporation's own annual period;[[37]](#cite_note-37) most Japanese corporations elect their annual period to follow the government fiscal year (1 April to 31 March).

### Lithuania

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Lithuania")]

In [Lithuania](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

### Macau

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Macau")]

In [Macau](/wiki/Macau "Macau"), the government's financial year is 1 January to 31 December.

### Malaysia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Malaysia")]

In [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"), the tax year for individuals is the calendar year, from 1 January to 31 December.[[39]](#cite_note-39)

The Companies Act 2016 does not state when the fiscal year must start for companies, so businesses are free to choose a financial year-end date.[[40]](#cite_note-40) Private businesses usually choose the last day of the calendar year or the last day of the quarter for their financial year end.

Generally, the government releases the annual [federal budget](/wiki/Malaysian_federal_budget "Malaysian federal budget") in October, ahead of the fiscal year.

### Mexico

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Mexico")]

In [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

### Moldova

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Moldova")]

In [Moldova](/wiki/Moldova "Moldova"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

### Myanmar/Burma

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Myanmar/Burma")]

In [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"), the fiscal year is 1 April to 31 March.[[42]](#cite_note-42)

### Nepal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Nepal")]

In [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal"), the fiscal year is 16 July (29 [Dilā](/wiki/Dila_(month) "Dila (month)") in [Nepal Sambat](/wiki/Nepal_Sambat "Nepal Sambat")) to 15 July (28 Dilā in Nepal Sambat).[[43]](#cite_note-43)

### New Zealand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: New Zealand")]

In [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand"), the government's fiscal[[44]](#cite_note-44) and financial reporting[[45]](#cite_note-45) year is 1 July to the next 30 June[[46]](#cite_note-46) and applies also to the budget. The company and personal financial year[[47]](#cite_note-47) is 1 April to 31 March and applies to company and personal income tax.

### Pakistan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Pakistan")]

In [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"), the government's fiscal year is 1 July of the previous calendar year and concludes on 30 June. Private companies are free to observe their own accounting year, which may not be the same as government's fiscal year.[[48]](#cite_note-Fund2012-48)

### Philippines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Philippines")]

In the [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"), the government's fiscal year is the calendar year, from 1 January to 31 December.[[49]](#cite_note-49)

The accounting period for the private sector must follow a 12-month fiscal period which can or can not be synchronized with the calendar year. Most Philippine companies end their fiscal years in December or March.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

### Poland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Poland")]

In [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, from 1 January to 31 December.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

### Portugal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Portugal")]

In [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

### Qatar

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Qatar")]

In [Qatar](/wiki/Qatar "Qatar"), the fiscal year is from 1 January to 31 December.

### Romania

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Romania")]

In [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

### Russia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Russia")]

In [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[22]](#cite_note-McKenna-22)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*]

### Saudi Arabia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Saudi Arabia")]

In [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[53]](#cite_note-53)

### Singapore

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Singapore")]

In [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore"), the fiscal year for the calculation of [personal income taxes](/wiki/Income_tax_in_Singapore "Income tax in Singapore") is 1 January to 31 December.[[54]](#cite_note-54)

The fiscal year for the [Government of Singapore](/wiki/Government_of_Singapore "Government of Singapore") and many [government-linked corporations](/wiki/Government-owned_corporation "Government-owned corporation") is 1 April to 31 March.[[4]](#cite_note-Fiscal_Year-4)

Corporations and organisations are permitted to select any date as the end of each fiscal year, as long as this date remains constant. However, new companies should consciously choose their financial year end to stretch as much as a duration of 12 months as possible.[[55]](#cite_note-55)

### South Africa

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: South Africa")]

In [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa"), the financial year for the [Government of South Africa](/wiki/Government_of_South_Africa "Government of South Africa") is 1 April to 31 March.[[4]](#cite_note-Fiscal_Year-4)

The year of assessment for individuals covers twelve months, 1 March to the final day of February the following year. The Act also provides for certain classes of taxpayers to have a year of assessment ending on a day other than the last day of February. Companies are permitted to have a tax year ending on a date that coincides with their financial year. Many older companies still use a tax year that runs from 1 July to 30 June, inherited from the British system. A common practice for newer companies is to run their tax year from 1 March to the final day of February following, to synchronize with the tax year for individuals.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### South Korea

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=45 "Edit section: South Korea")]

In [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[56]](#cite_note-56)

### Spain

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=46 "Edit section: Spain")]

In [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[57]](#cite_note-57)

### Sweden

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=47 "Edit section: Sweden")]

In [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), the fiscal year for individuals is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[58]](#cite_note-58)

The fiscal year for an organisation is typically one of the following:

* 1 January to 31 December
* 1 May to 30 April
* 1 July to 30 June
* 1 September to 31 August

However, all calendar months are allowed. If an organisation wishes to change into a non-calendar year, permission from the [Tax Authority](/wiki/Swedish_Tax_Agency "Swedish Tax Agency") is required.[[59]](#cite_note-59)[[60]](#cite_note-60)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=48 "Edit section: Switzerland")]

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=49 "Edit section: Taiwan")]

In [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December. However, an enterprise may elect to adopt a special fiscal year at the time it is established and can request approval from the tax authorities to change its fiscal year.[[62]](#cite_note-62)

### Thailand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=50 "Edit section: Thailand")]

In [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"), the government's fiscal year (FY) is 1 October to 30 September of the following year.[[63]](#cite_note-63) For individual taxpayers it is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=51 "Edit section: Turkey")]

In [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[64]](#cite_note-64)

### Ukraine

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=52 "Edit section: Ukraine")]

In [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[65]](#cite_note-65)

### United Arab Emirates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=53 "Edit section: United Arab Emirates")]

In the [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.[[4]](#cite_note-Fiscal_Year-4)

### United Kingdom

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=54 "Edit section: United Kingdom")]

In the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), the financial year runs from 1 April to 31 March for the purposes of government financial statements.[[5]](#cite_note-Interpretation_Act_1978-5) For personal tax purposes the fiscal year starts on 6 April and ends on 5 April of the next calendar year.[[66]](#cite_note-66)

Although [United Kingdom corporation tax](/wiki/United_Kingdom_corporation_tax "United Kingdom corporation tax") is charged by reference to the government's financial year, companies can adopt any year as their accounting year: if there is a change in tax rate, the taxable profit is apportioned to financial years on a time basis.[[67]](#cite_note-67)

A number of major corporations that were once government-owned, such as [BT Group](/wiki/BT_Group "BT Group") and the [National Grid](/wiki/National_Grid_(UK) "National Grid (UK)"), continue to use the government's financial year, which ends on the last day of March, as they have found no reason to change since [privatisation](/wiki/Privatisation "Privatisation").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The 5 April year end for income tax reflects the old civil and [ecclesiastical calendar](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_calendar "Ecclesiastical calendar") under which New Year began on 25 March (*[Lady Day](/wiki/Lady_Day "Lady Day")*). The difference between the two dates is accounted for by the eleven days omitted in September 1752 due to the [Calendar (New Style) Act 1750](/wiki/Calendar_(New_Style)_Act_1750 "Calendar (New Style) Act 1750") by which [Great Britain](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Great_Britain "Kingdom of Great Britain") also converted from the [Julian Calendar](/wiki/Julian_Calendar "Julian Calendar") to the [Gregorian Calendar](/wiki/Gregorian_Calendar "Gregorian Calendar"). However, although the calendar year finished on 24 March, the tax year finished a day later, on 25 March, the [Quarter Day](/wiki/Quarter_Day "Quarter Day") – the traditional day on which debts were settled. (For a fuller explanation about the history of the United Kingdom income tax year and its start date, see [History of taxation in the United Kingdom § Start of tax year](/wiki/History_of_taxation_in_the_United_Kingdom#Start_of_tax_year "History of taxation in the United Kingdom").)

### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=55 "Edit section: United States")]

#### Federal government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=56 "Edit section: Federal government")]

In the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), the fiscal year of the [federal government](/wiki/Federal_government_of_the_United_States "Federal government of the United States") begins 1 October and ends 30 September the following year. The current fiscal year is the fiscal year 2026 and is abbreviated FY26 or FY2025-26. It began 1 October 2025 and will end on 30 September 2026.[[68]](#cite_note-:0-68)

For example, the United States government fiscal year 2025-26 is:

United States government fiscal year 2025-26

| Quarter | Dates |
| --- | --- |
| 1st | 1 October 2025 – 31 December 2025 |
| 2nd | 1 January 2026 – 31 March 2026 |
| 3rd | 1 April 2026 – 30 June 2026 |
| 4th | 1 July 2026 – 30 September 2026 |

Source:[[68]](#cite_note-:0-68)

Fiscal years are used by the United States federal government for accounting purposes.[[69]](#cite_note-69)[*[further explanation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*]

In 1843, the federal government changed the fiscal year from that of a calendar year (beginning 1 January), to one starting on 1 July.[[70]](#cite_note-70) These fiscal years ran from 1 July to 30 June the following year. The current fiscal year of 1 October to 30 September was introduced by the [Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974](/wiki/Congressional_Budget_and_Impoundment_Control_Act_of_1974 "Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974"). This created what is known as the "transitional quarter", from 1 July 1976 to 30 September 1976, to allow Congress more time to arrive at budget decisions. The first "modern" fiscal year ran 1 October 1976 to 30 September 1977, and was called fiscal year 1976-77 (abbreviated FY77).**[[71]](#cite_note-:1-71)**

United States government fiscal year 1975-76 and transition period
(Historical)

| Quarter | Dates |
| --- | --- |
| 1st | 1 July 1975 – 30 September 1975 |
| 2nd | 1 October 1975 – 31 December 1975 |
| 3rd | 1 January 1976 – 31 March 1976 |
| 4th | 1 April 1976 – 30 June 1976 |
| *Plus* | *1 July 1976 – 30 September 1976* |
| *FY1977* | *1 October 1976 – 30 September 1977* |

Source:**[[71]](#cite_note-:1-71)**

#### State governments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=57 "Edit section: State governments")]

[State governments](/wiki/State_governments_of_the_United_States "State governments of the United States") set their own fiscal year. Forty-six of the fifty states set their fiscal year to end on 30 June.[[72]](#cite_note-NCSL-72) Two states have fiscal years that are different:

* [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), ends 31 March[[73]](#cite_note-73)
* [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas"), ends 31 August

The fiscal year for the [Washington, DC](/wiki/Washington,_DC "Washington, DC") government ends on 30 September.[[74]](#cite_note-74)

Among the inhabited [territories of the United States](/wiki/Territories_of_the_United_States "Territories of the United States"), most align with the federal fiscal year, ending on 30 September. These include [American Samoa](/wiki/American_Samoa "American Samoa"), [Guam](/wiki/Guam "Guam"), the [Northern Mariana Islands](/wiki/Northern_Mariana_Islands "Northern Mariana Islands") and the [US Virgin Islands](/wiki/United_States_Virgin_Islands "United States Virgin Islands").[[72]](#cite_note-NCSL-72) [Puerto Rico](/wiki/Puerto_Rico "Puerto Rico") is the exception, with its fiscal year ending on 30 June.

### Vietnam

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=58 "Edit section: Vietnam")]

In [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam"), the fiscal year is the calendar year, 1 January to 31 December.

## Businesses and organizations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fiscal_year&action=edit&section=59 "Edit section: Businesses and organizations")]

The tax year for a business is governed by the fiscal year it chooses. A business may choose any consistent fiscal year that it wants; however, for seasonal businesses such as farming and retail, a good accounting practice is to end the fiscal year shortly after the highest revenue time of year. Consequently, most large agriculture companies end their fiscal years after the harvest season, and most retailers end their fiscal years shortly after the Christmas shopping season. Economist Pamela P. Drake notes that there are cases where businesses choose a year-end which fits with the slower part of their business year. At this point in the year they are likely to hold less [inventory](/wiki/Inventory "Inventory") than their average daily inventory over the whole year.[[75]](#cite_note-75)
